title: Conversation summaries from meeting transcripts
use_when: Conversation summaries from meeting transcripts. Use when the user needs a product workflow for project management related to conversation summaries from meeting transcripts. Trigger terms: meetings, summarization, note-taking, communication.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Conversation summaries from meeting transcripts when the request is mainly technical implementation, analytics modeling, or UX critique; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: Conversation summaries from meeting transcripts stakeholder narrative with audience, message, risks, asks, and follow-up owner
framework_fit: Conversation summaries from meeting transcripts fits Operations work in the Align lifecycle when the user needs Conversation summaries from meeting transcripts stakeholder narrative with audience, message, risks, asks, and follow-up owner and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces Conversation summaries from meeting transcripts stakeholder narrative with audience, message, risks, asks, and follow-up owner without tying recommendations to the user's Align stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Conversation summaries from meeting transcripts as generic Operations advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Conversation summaries from meeting transcripts when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $conversation-summaries-from-meeting-transcripts to turn this conversation context into a decision-ready Conversation summaries from meeting transcripts stakeholder narrative with audience, message, risks, asks, and follow-up owner.
examples.expected_artifact: Conversation summaries from meeting transcripts stakeholder narrative with audience, message, risks, asks, and follow-up owner
